evals.prompt: How do I set up a multichannel sequence in Reply.io with email and LinkedIn steps?
evals.expected_output: Guide to creating a multichannel sequence with email and LinkedIn steps, including add-on requirements and branching logic

assertions.name: sequence_setup_steps
assertions.description: Provides step-by-step instructions for creating a sequence and adding email and LinkedIn steps in the sequence builder

assertions.name: linkedin_addon_requirement
assertions.description: Mentions that LinkedIn automation requires the $69/account add-on and is not included in the base Multichannel plan

assertions.name: conditional_branching
assertions.description: Explains conditional branching — routing prospects down different paths based on behavior (e.g., opened email vs. did not open)

evals.prompt: How do I configure Reply.io's email warmup and check my domain health?
evals.expected_output: Guide to enabling built-in warmup, monitoring domain health, and integrating with Google Postmaster

assertions.name: warmup_setup_steps
assertions.description: Provides steps to enable Reply.io's built-in peer-to-peer warmup on connected mailboxes and recommends 2-3 week warmup period

assertions.name: domain_health_checker
assertions.description: Mentions the domain health checker tab in Reply.io for monitoring domain reputation and configuration

assertions.name: google_postmaster
assertions.description: References Google Postmaster API integration for monitoring spam rate and domain reputation

evals.prompt: How do I use the Reply.io API to programmatically add contacts to a sequence?
evals.expected_output: API guidance for adding contacts to a sequence with authentication and endpoint details

assertions.name: api_reference_pointer
assertions.description: Points to references/reply-api-reference.md for full API documentation

assertions.name: endpoint_path
assertions.description: References the POST /sequences/:id/contacts endpoint for adding contacts to a sequence
